title: hieracium sylvaticum subsp. hugeliense
intro: Hieracium sylvaticum subsp. hugeliense, commonly known as the alpine hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and inflammation. The plant's leaves and flowers contain sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, providing potential health benefits such as wound healing and immune system support. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and ointments to harness its medicinal properties, although its use should be guided by a healthcare professional due to potential interactions and side effects.
